mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, allowing users to create and manage AI-driven applications. It is ideal for teams looking to leverage AI capabilities in their workflows while maintaining control over their infrastructure. Key trade-offs include the need for self-hosting expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dify into the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Change the version as needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to demonstrate Dify's functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This creates a simple Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dify application to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp -n dify

```

> You should see the example-dify-app listed with a status of 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dify and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to Dify applications.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command removes the Dify release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp -n dify -o yaml > backup-difyapp.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get difyapp -n dify

```

> Backup existing Dify applications.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the Dify application is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get difyapp -n dify -o yaml > backup-difyapp.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyApp not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Dify application does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the name of the Dify application and ensure it was created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dify service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n dify' to check the status of the pods and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule Dify pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm installation may have encountered an issue due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status dify --namespace dify' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow features and improved API stability.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.11, review the new API documentation for changes in resource definitions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify application listed with a status of 'Running' when you execute 'kubectl get difyapp -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
